methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 6 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Q-CTRL, a Series B quantum computing company founded by Michael Biercuk, was last valued at $500M post-money on $132M in total capital raised. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Valuation analysis relied on stage-progression methodology, which proved the only applicable triangulation approach. The peer-median method was not viable due to insufficient comparable data points at Q-CTRL's specific stage and valuation tier; while 6 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 qualified under narrow same-stage or adjacent-stage filters, falling below the minimum threshold for reliable cross-sectional analysis. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier—typical for Series B to next-stage advancement—to the $500M last post-money valuation, yielding $950M.

Given the single-method result, the triangulated range is anchored at $950M (median), with low and high bounds of $808M and $1.1B respectively, derived from ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 1.9x multiplier to quantum computing's specific risk and growth profile, and the absence of direct peer transaction evidence at this valuation tier. The narrow methodological base suggests caution in treating this range as anything other than illustrative pending additional comparable data or revenue-multiple frameworks.
